text:
Indian wrestler Antim Panghal is set to be flown home from theOlympicsafter she was involved in a major ‘disciplinary breach’ at the athletes’ village inParis.

Panghal was seen as a medal hope in the women’s 53kg wrestling competition but suffered an emphatic loss to Turkey’s Yetgil Zeynep in the first round on Wednesday.

However, the 19-year-old’s maiden Olympics went from bad to worse after her sister was detained by French police for trying to enter the athletes’ village with her accreditation.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

First reported by Indian outletWorld In News,Panghal’s sister, Nisha, was denied entry to the village by security before being escorted to a police station.

She was later released after help from India’s chef de mission in Paris but the incident has now led to the Indian Olympic Association deciding to fly the wrestler – and her extended team – home from the Games.

‘The Indian Olympic Association has decided to fly wrestler Antim and her support staff back after a disciplinary breach was brought to IOA’s notice by the French authorities,’ a statement from the association said.

Panghal, meanwhile, has responded to the claims, saying she felt unwell and her sister was merely trying to collect her belongings while she rested up in the hotel where her team were staying.

‘Since I’ve lost, talks are going on that my sister has been arrested by the police, or I’ve been arrested by the police, nothing of that sort has happened,’ she said in a video.

‘When I lost my bout yesterday, I got a fever. My sister, who has been living in a hotel, wanted to take me there. I had taken permission from my coaches to go with her, as I wasn’t feeling well.

‘They gave me permission and I came to the hotel. When I came here, I needed some of my stuff, which was still in the Olympic Village. As I was unwell, I slept. My sister took my accreditation card and went to the village. She asked the authorities there if she was allowed to get my stuff from the village.

‘The authorities took away the accreditation card from her and took her to the police station for verification. This was the only reason she was taken to the police station.’

Reports in India also claim that one of Panghal’s coaches got into a heated dispute with a taxi driver in Paris, something the wrestling also denies.

‘My coaches were just upset with my loss. I was the one who booked their cab. They came to the hotel but got into a bit of trouble because of a language barrier,’ she added.

‘They wanted to come to the hotel to collect money [Euros] but some miscommunication happened with the cab driver.

‘One of my coaches came to the room to collect money and since the room was high up in the building, it took some time. The delay resulted in a bit of an argument with the cab driver, but nothing major.’

title: Olympic wrestler flown home after 'disciplinary breach' at Olympic village | Metro News
description: Indian wrestler Antim Panghal is set to be flown home from the Olympics after a 'disciplinary breach' at the athletes' village in Paris.
